title: Upon Paske, A Draper.
content:
Paske, though his debt be due upon the day
    Demands no money by a craving way;
    For why, says he, all debts and their arrears
    Have reference to the shoulders, not the ears.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon Paske, A Draper.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
